perturbation.original_text: For purposes of this Agreement, the term “ Evaluation Material ” shall mean any and all information provided to Investor by or on behalf of the Company in connection with the Transaction whether in writing, orally, visually, electronically or in machine readable form, through demonstration or in any other manner, and shall include, without limitation, any information regarding or relating to the Company’s operations, business opportunities, costs, finances, personnel, marketing and promotion plans, financial statements, financial information and business practices or policies, and any other business information of the Company or the terms or existence of this Agreement, the Company’s consideration of a possible Transaction or any discussions between the parties with respect thereto, transmitted by or on behalf of the Company to Investor in connection with the Transaction, whether prior to or after the execution and delivery of this Agreement and whether or not such information is marked “confidential”.
perturbation.changed_text: For purposes of this Agreement, the term “ Evaluation Material ” shall mean any and all data provided to Investor by or on behalf of the Company in connection with the Transaction whether in writing, orally, visually, electronically or in machine readable form, through demonstration or in any other manner, and shall include, without limitation, any information regarding or relating to the Company’s operations, business opportunities, costs, finances, personnel, marketing and promotion plans, financial statements, financial information and business practices or policies, and any other business information of the Company or the terms or existence of this Agreement, the Company’s consideration of a possible Transaction or any discussions between the parties with respect thereto, transmitted by or on behalf of the Company to Investor in connection with the Transaction, whether prior to or after the execution and delivery of this Agreement and whether or not such information is marked “confidential”.
perturbation.explanation: The change from "information" to "data" within the definition of 'Evaluation Material' introduces a contradiction. The rest of the definition uses 'information,' implying that 'Evaluation Material' only refers to data, and the rest of the text would contradict the meaning since it's saying it's information. This creates uncertainty as to whether documents, presentations, or other forms of information fall under the confidentiality obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Evaluation Material . For purposes of this Agreement, the term “ Evaluation Material ” shall mean any and all data provided to Investor by or on behalf of the Company in connection with the Transaction whether in writing, orally, visually, electronically or in machine readable form, through demonstration or in any other manner, and shall include, without limitation, any information regarding or relating to the Company’s operations, business opportunities, costs, finances, personnel, marketing and promotion plans, financial statements, financial information and business practices or policies, and any other business information of the Company or the terms or existence of this Agreement, the Company’s consideration of a possible Transaction or any discussions between the parties with respect thereto, transmitted by or on behalf of the Company to Investor in connection with the Transaction, whether prior to or after the execution and delivery of this Agreement and whether or not such information is marked “confidential”.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters "information" to "data" in the definition of 'Evaluation Material', while the rest of the definition and the contradicted text uses "information". This creates a contradiction because 'data' and 'information' are used interchangeably in the definition of 'Evaluation Material', leading to conflicting obligations, according to the definition of Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction, which says it leads to in-text contradictions when the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations. Since the definition of evaluation material uses both data and information while calling it Evaluation Material, it creates a direct conflict that leads to uncertainty over which term should be enforced.

perturbation.original_text: The Evaluation Material will be used by Investor solely in connection with its evaluation of a possible Transaction, and not for any other purpose whatsoever.
perturbation.changed_text: The Confidential Data will be used by Investor solely in connection with its evaluation of a possible Transaction, and not for any other purpose whatsoever.
perturbation.explanation: By changing "Evaluation Material" to "Confidential Data," without explicitly defining "Confidential Data", it creates confusion. It's unclear whether 'Evaluation Material', as originally defined in Section 1, and 'Confidential Data' are the same, different or related. The definition of Evaluation Material is not used to reference Confidential Data, this creates contradiction and uncertainty on which term should apply.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Use of Evaluation Material . The Confidential Data will be used by Investor solely in connection with its evaluation of a possible Transaction, and not for any other purpose whatsoever. The Evaluation Material will be kept confidential by Investor in accordance with the terms and conditions of this Agreement and will not be disclosed to any person, except that Investor may disclose the Evaluation Material or portions thereof to those of its employees, officers, directors, partners, members, affiliates, accountants, attorneys, agents, consultants and advisors (these persons being collectively called “ Representatives ”) who (a) need to know such information in order for Investor to be able to evaluate a possible Transaction and (b) have been directed by Investor to treat the Evaluation Material in accordance with the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text used 'Evaluation Material' which is defined elsewhere in the document. The changed text replaces 'Evaluation Material' with 'Confidential Data' without defining 'Confidential Data' or clarifying its relationship to 'Evaluation Material'. The 'Contradicted Text' uses both terms, creating a strong contradiction and uncertainty regarding which term applies and to what extent the obligations related to each term apply. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition, which states that using multiple terms interchangeably without defining them leads to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: If Investor or its Representatives receive a subpoena or other validly issued administrative or judicial process requesting Evaluation Material, or if Investor or its Representatives are requested or required to disclose any Evaluation Material under law, governmental order, judicial order, regulatory or statutory requirement, discovery request, oral questions, interrogatories, requests for information or documents, subpoena, civil investigative demand or similar process, Investor shall notify the Company reasonably promptly following Investor or its Representatives first becoming aware of such requested disclosure, so that the Company may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy or, in the Company’s sole discretion, waive compliance with the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: If Investor or its Representatives receive a subpoena or other validly issued administrative or judicial process requesting Confidential Records, or if Investor or its Representatives are requested or required to disclose any Evaluation Material under law, governmental order, judicial order, regulatory or statutory requirement, discovery request, oral questions, interrogatories, requests for information or documents, subpoena, civil investigative demand or similar process, Investor shall notify the Company reasonably promptly following Investor or its Representatives first becoming aware of such requested disclosure, so that the Company may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy or, in the Company’s sole discretion, waive compliance with the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Evaluation Material" to "Confidential Records" only in the context of a subpoena request creates ambiguity. It is undefined what "Confidential Records" are, it's unclear if the subpoena request applies to all Evaluation Material, some subset of it, or something entirely different, causing confusion about the investor's obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Exceptions . If Investor or its Representatives receive a subpoena or other validly issued administrative or judicial process requesting Confidential Records, or if Investor or its Representatives are requested or required to disclose any Evaluation Material under law, governmental order, judicial order, regulatory or statutory requirement, discovery request, oral questions, interrogatories, requests for information or documents, subpoena, civil investigative demand or similar process, Investor shall notify the Company reasonably promptly following Investor or its Representatives first becoming aware of such requested disclosure, so that the Company may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy or, in the Company’s sole discretion, waive compliance with the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change from "Evaluation Material" to "Confidential Records" in the context of a subpoena request introduces ambiguity and creates a contradiction. While the contradicted text also refers to both "Confidential Records" and "Evaluation Material" in the subpoena context, the change in the original text now makes it unclear whether a subpoena request for "Confidential Records" also necessitates the same notification and protective action regarding "Evaluation Material", and vice versa. This is because "Confidential Records" is undefined, thus directly contradicting the other part of the document and creating uncertainty.

perturbation.original_text: At any time upon the written request of the Company, Investor will, at its own expense, (i) reasonably promptly redeliver to the Company or, at the at Investor’s option, destroy all copies (including permanently erasing or deleting any electronic copies) of the Evaluation Material in the possession or control of Investor or any of its Representatives and (ii) reasonably promptly destroy all analyses, notes, data, compilations, summaries, forecasts, studies or other documents and materials prepared by Investor or any of its Representatives in connection with their evaluation and review of a possible Transaction that contain, reflect or are based upon or generated from, in whole or in part, any of the Evaluation Material.
perturbation.changed_text: At any time upon the written request of the Company, Investor will, at its own expense, (i) reasonably promptly redeliver to the Company or, at the at Investor’s option, destroy all copies (including permanently erasing or deleting any electronic copies) of the Confidential Documentation in the possession or control of Investor or any of its Representatives and (ii) reasonably promptly destroy all analyses, notes, data, compilations, summaries, forecasts, studies or other documents and materials prepared by Investor or any of its Representatives in connection with their evaluation and review of a possible Transaction that contain, reflect or are based upon or generated from, in whole or in part, any of the Evaluation Material.
perturbation.explanation: The conflicting terminology between "Confidential Documentation" and "Evaluation Material" regarding the return/destruction of material creates an inconsistency. The use of "Confidential Documentation" is introduced without definition. It causes confusion as to which standard applies to returning any remaining documentation, since its not defined, it's not clarified what is considered Confidential Documentation. This affects all documentation, whether it's "Confidential Documentation" or "Evaluation Material".
perturbation.contradicted_text: Return of Evaluation Material . At any time upon the written request of the Company, Investor will, at its own expense, (i) reasonably promptly redeliver to the Company or, at the at Investor’s option, destroy all copies (including permanently erasing or deleting any electronic copies) of the Confidential Documentation in the possession or control of Investor or any of its Representatives and (ii) reasonably promptly destroy all analyses, notes, data, compilations, summaries, forecasts, studies or other documents and materials prepared by Investor or any of its Representatives in connection with their evaluation and review of a possible Transaction that contain, reflect or are based upon or generated from, in whole or in part, any of the Evaluation Material.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Evaluation Material' with 'Confidential Documentation' in the section detailing the return or destruction of materials. The 'Contradicted Text' retains the definition of the return of 'Evaluation Material', but the 'Changed Text' now uses 'Confidential Documentation' without defining it. This creates a conflict because it's unclear whether the instructions apply to all materials, or if there is a separate process for returning 'Evaluation Material'. This misaligned terminology leads to in-text contradiction per the provided definition. The contradiction is strong because it makes it unclear what must be returned, which would cause a significant legal issue if there was a dispute about returning documents.
